input.instructions: Design an optimal querying strategy to identify hidden polyominoes on a grid. Given a set of known polyomino shapes and a noise parameter, determine how to use single-cell queries (q 1 x y) and multi-cell aggregate queries (q k x1 y1 ...) to minimize total cost. Focus on how to use aggregate queries to detect presence/absence of oil in regions and how to refine locations using single-cell drills. Provide a mathematical approach for deciding when to switch from aggregate to single-cell queries based on the noise parameter and the known shapes.

variables.selected_prompt_text:
Solve the question directly.

Keep decision_notes extremely short. Prefer 3 to 8 words.

JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>
Keep the justification to 12 words or fewer.
Use plain text only inside the response field. Do not use markdown formatting, code fences, or math-style backslashes such as \equiv.
Do not add any extra text before or after the JSON object.

Return exactly one JSON object in this format:
{
  "schema_name": "eywa_node_response",

  "decision_notes": "optional string",

}

Question:
Design an optimal querying strategy to identify hidden polyominoes on a grid. Given a set of known polyomino shapes and a noise parameter, determine how to use single-cell queries (q 1 x y) and multi-cell aggregate queries (q k x1 y1 ...) to minimize total cost. Focus on how to use aggregate queries to detect presence/absence of oil in regions and how to refine locations using single-cell drills. Provide a mathematical approach for deciding when to switch from aggregate to single-cell queries based on the noise parameter and the known shapes.

results.content:
FINAL_ANSWER: Use hierarchical spatial decomposition with adaptive thresholding.
JUSTIFICATION: Aggregate queries prune empty space; single-cell queries refine candidates.
